verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted cysteine-rich protein with no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or documented post-translational TM-partner tethering. It is secreted into extracellular space and seminal plasma, existing as a soluble protein that transiently binds cell-surface receptors via reversible non-covalent interaction. No cancer ecto-pool, cell-death display, activation-induced surfacing, or constitutive/cycling PM pool is documented. Fails the wash test: no stable anchoring to the membrane. Classical secreted_only.
